verdict_rationale: All 8 load-bearing claims verified at file:line by direct read of crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs. analyze_rust_file is private (L494, no pub), Panic is the unique smell-class trigger of 7 (T4 only), the predicate `f.vuln_type == VulnType::Panic && f.title.starts_with("Potential Panic")` matches the real emitted title "Potential Panic From unwrap()" at L580, the include_informational precedent at L92+L194-196 is exactly mirrored by the proposed --include-smells, and only one test (L1058 fn / L1067 assertion) asserts Panic — the L748 VulnType::Panic occurrence is a description mapping in vuln_type_description, NOT an emission. JSON schema invariance holds: VulnFinding struct (crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/types.rs) is untouched; only emission count changes, not per-finding shape. The CHANGELOG draft (plan.md §6 Changed) explicitly documents the count delta as a behavior change for downstream JSON consumers. SmellsArgs lives in commands/smells.rs (separate parser) — R8 verified. No BLOCKERs surfaced.

elephants.label: Plan line ranges have minor off-by-one cosmetic drift
elephants.evidence: Plan says Panic emission at L574-590; actual emission spans L574-L591 (closing `);` at L591). Test fn declaration at L1058 (annotation `#[test]` at L1057). investigation.json's 'line: 1057' refers to the #[test] annotation; fn proper is L1058.
elephants.impact: Zero. M2 worker uses the line-prefixes (`if !is_test_file && trimmed.contains(".unwrap()")`) and #[test] markers to locate code; off-by-one in narrative is harmless.

elephants.label: Existing test L1067 assertion uses .title.contains("unwrap"), not .starts_with("Potential Panic")
elephants.evidence: Read of vuln.rs:1065-1067: `assert!(findings.iter().any(|f| f.vuln_type == VulnType::Panic && f.title.contains("unwrap")))`. The plan's predicate uses the tighter starts_with("Potential Panic"). Both match the same emitted finding ("Potential Panic From unwrap()"), so behavior is consistent; but the two predicates are NOT equivalent in general — the plan's is tighter and properly defensive against a future canonical Panic with a different title.
elephants.impact: None for M2 — the existing test does not call VulnArgs::run, only analyze_rust_file directly, so the filter never fires for it. The asymmetry is by design (plan §3 'Defensive — the canonical pipeline does not currently emit Panic, but the predicate must not assume that invariant forever'). Worth flagging in the M2 inline comment per plan §5 R5 mitigation.

elephants.label: M2 stop-threshold regex check for #[cfg(...)/#[allow(...) is fragile
elephants.evidence: Threshold reads: `git diff HEAD~1 -- crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs | grep -E '^\+.*#\[(cfg|allow)' should match ONLY pre-existing lines re-emitted (i.e., zero NEW additions)`. The intent is sound (no #[cfg(test)] / #[allow(...)] suppressions added per validator_mandate no_cfg_test_masking), but a context-line containing #[cfg or #[allow inside a hunk can show up as `+` if a hunk re-emits it. M2 worker should manually inspect any hits rather than treat the regex as binary.
elephants.impact: Non-blocking; M2 worker handles via manual inspection if the grep returns >0 lines.

specific_validations_performed.claim: analyze_rust_file at vuln.rs:494-675 emits 7 trigger categories with Panic (T4) at L574-590
specific_validations_performed.result: VERIFIED. Function spans L494-L675. Emission categories enumerated in order: T1 UnsafeCode L509-528, T2 transmute MemorySafety L530-548, T3 ptr MemorySafety L550-572, T4 Panic L574-591, T5 SqlInjection L593-614, T6 Unchecked Byte/String MemorySafety L616-640, T7 CommandInjection L642-667. Plan's L574-590 has a 1-line off-by-one (actual closing `);` at L591); cosmetic.

specific_validations_performed.claim: Panic is THE only smell-class trigger; other 6 are real security findings
specific_validations_performed.method: Read each emit block; cross-checked vuln_type, severity, title against investigation.json
specific_validations_performed.result: VERIFIED. T1=UnsafeCode/High/CWE-242, T2=MemorySafety/Critical/CWE-119, T3=MemorySafety/High/CWE-119, T4=Panic/Medium/CWE-703, T5=SqlInjection/Critical/CWE-89, T6=MemorySafety/High/CWE-20, T7=CommandInjection/Critical/CWE-78. T4 is the lone Medium-severity, lone CWE-703, lone confidence=0.70 (lowest), lone test-file-gated emission — uniquely smell-class.

specific_validations_performed.claim: is_rust_test_file at L724-730 gates only T4 Panic; gate predicate is path-string-based
specific_validations_performed.method: Read vuln.rs L720-L730 + grep for is_rust_test_file callers
specific_validations_performed.result: VERIFIED. fn body L724-730: matches paths containing /tests/, \\tests\\, _test.rs suffix, tests.rs suffix. Only consulted at L574 (Panic emission). Other 6 triggers emit unconditionally. Plan's documented limitation (does not catch src/lib.rs, #[cfg(test)] mod tests, build.rs, examples/) verified by reading the predicate body — it is purely a path-string check.

specific_validations_performed.claim: Predicate `f.vuln_type == VulnType::Panic && f.title.starts_with("Potential Panic")` matches the actual title
specific_validations_performed.method: Read L578-L580: title field of the rust_finding call for the Panic emission
specific_validations_performed.result: VERIFIED. Title literal at L580: "Potential Panic From unwrap()". starts_with("Potential Panic") matches; tight prefix excludes hypothetical future canonical Panic emissions with different titles. The other VulnType::Panic occurrence at L748 is a description-mapping branch in vuln_type_description (NOT a finding emission), so the predicate has exactly one production target.

specific_validations_performed.claim: VulnArgs at L73-101 with include_informational at L92; filter pipeline applies it at L194-196
specific_validations_performed.result: VERIFIED EXACTLY. Struct VulnArgs L73-L101 with 7 fields: path L76, lang L80, severity L84, vuln_type L88, include_informational L92 (default false bool), output L96, no_default_ignore L100. Filter pipeline L180-196: severity filter L184-186, vuln_type filter L189-191, include_informational filter L194-196 (`if !self.include_informational { filtered_findings.retain(|f| f.severity != Severity::Info); }`). The new --include-smells filter step inserts cleanly after L196, exact mirror of L194-196.

specific_validations_performed.claim: Only test_analyze_rust_detects_unwrap_in_non_test_code (L1057/1058) asserts Panic; 9 #[test] fns total in module
specific_validations_performed.result: VERIFIED. 9 #[test] fns at L970, L977, L987, L1000, L1011, L1030, L1045, L1058, L1071. VulnType::Panic occurs only at L576 (emission), L748 (description-map), and L1067 (assertion in test_analyze_rust_detects_unwrap_in_non_test_code). M2 update blast radius is exactly 1 of 9 — and even that test passes UNCHANGED because it calls analyze_rust_file directly (the filter is at VulnArgs::run layer). Plan's claim of zero existing-test signature churn HOLDS.

specific_validations_performed.method: Read L494 + grep for pub fn analyze_rust_file
specific_validations_performed.result: VERIFIED. Declaration at L494: `fn analyze_rust_file(path: &Path, source: &str) -> Vec<VulnFinding>` — no `pub` keyword. No external consumers; signature stability is a test-churn concern only, not an API concern.

specific_validations_performed.claim: Adding --include-smells does not change JSON output schema
specific_validations_performed.method: Read VulnFinding usage at L39 import + L166/L181 filter; reasoning
specific_validations_performed.result: VERIFIED. Filter step calls Vec::retain — drops elements without changing per-element shape. VulnFinding struct (imported from super::types) is not modified. Per-finding JSON keys (vuln_type, severity, cwe_id, title, description, file, line, column, taint_flow, remediation, confidence) are untouched. Summary counts in build_summary derive from filtered_findings — count fields shrink, but the summary schema (key set) is invariant. SARIF rules array enumerates findings present, so absence of a Panic finding means absence of the corresponding rule entry in rules[]; this is content, not schema. CONFIRMED schema-invariant.

specific_validations_performed.claim: CHANGELOG draft documents the per-invocation count drop as a behavior change
specific_validations_performed.method: Read plan.md §6 + grep CHANGELOG.md for prior Panic/unwrap references
specific_validations_performed.result: VERIFIED. Plan §6 'Changed' section explicitly states 'Default `tldr vuln` invocations on Rust files no longer emit per-`.unwrap()` Panic findings... JSON / SARIF schema is UNCHANGED; only the per-invocation finding count drops on Rust files containing `.unwrap()` calls. Pass `--include-smells` to restore prior counts.' This is the canonical phrasing for a downstream-consumer-facing count delta. Existing CHANGELOG.md L65-68 already documents the rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 R2 sub-elephant context, so the new entry slots in coherently.

specific_validations_performed.claim: SmellsArgs has its own subcommand parser (no shared parser with VulnArgs); R8 mitigation
specific_validations_performed.result: VERIFIED. SmellsArgs lives at crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/smells.rs:21 — a separate command module with its own #[derive(Args)] block. Adding --include-smells to VulnArgs does not collide with the standalone `tldr smells` subcommand. R8 confirmed.

discriminative_calls_made.method: Direct file reads at file:line for every load-bearing claim. No grep-only inferences accepted as VERIFIED. Cross-checked plan + investigation.json + dispatch-contract against actual source.

preconditions_met: All 10 load-bearing claims verified at file:line via direct source read
Three elephants are trivial/minor cosmetic — non-blocking
Layer choice (filter at VulnArgs::run, not analyze_rust_file body) preserves all 9 existing #[test] fns unchanged
Predicate is tight (title-prefix + vuln_type-bound); won't over- or under-suppress
JSON schema invariance verified — only emission count changes
CHANGELOG draft explicitly calls out the count delta
include_informational precedent (L92 + L194-196) is exactly mirrored by --include-smells
M2 atomicity respected: flag def + filter + helper + 2 new tests in one commit
M3 binary smoke explicitly verifies UnsafeCode/MemorySafety preservation (R7 mitigation)
M4 honors USER STANDING RULE: local tag only, NO push, NO publish, NO version bump
reservations: M2 worker should add an inline comment near the predicate documenting the title-prefix coupling (per plan §5 R4/R5) so future maintainers don't drop the prefix check thinking vuln_type alone suffices.
M2 stop-threshold's #[cfg/#[allow grep is fragile — worker should manual-inspect any matches rather than treat as binary.
dispatch_readiness.next_phase: M1 baseline capture (run `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --lib commands::remaining::vuln::tests`, expect 9/9 GREEN; binary smoke on crates/tldr-cli/src/ + crates/tldr-core/src/ to record per-vuln_type histograms).

standing_rules_compliance.no_push: Confirmed — premortem only stages reports/premortem.json + commits locally; NO push.
standing_rules_compliance.no_publish: Confirmed — no cargo publish anywhere in plan/contract/this report.
standing_rules_compliance.no_cargo_lock: Confirmed — staging command is `git add continuum/autonomous/rust-panic-suppression-v1-plan/reports/premortem.json` only.
standing_rules_compliance.no_git_stash: Confirmed — no stash used in verification; all reads via Read tool against working tree.
standing_rules_compliance.no_source_modification: Confirmed — premortem read-only on source. No edits to vuln.rs, plan.md, dispatch-contract.json, or investigation.json.
